sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, description) VALUES (1, 'hello world', 'first entry'), (2, NULL, 'second entry'), (3, '', 'third entry');
SELECT id, initcap(name) AS capitalized_name FROM test_table WHERE NOT (name IS NULL OR name = '');
SELECT id, initcap(description) AS capitalized_description FROM test_table WHERE NOT description IS NULL;
SELECT id, initcap(name) AS capitalized_name FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE NOT (name IS NULL) AND LENGTH(name) > 0);
SELECT COUNT(*), initcap(name) FROM test_table GROUP BY initcap(name) HAVING NOT COUNT(name) = 0;
SELECT name FROM test_table JOIN (SELECT id, initcap(name) AS capitalized_name FROM test_table WHERE NOT name IS NULL) AS tmp ON test_table.id = tmp.id WHERE NOT (length(test_table.name) = 0);
SELECT id, name, (CASE WHEN created_at IS NULL THEN 'No Date' ELSE initcap(TEXT(created_at)) END) AS formatted_date FROM test_table WHERE NOT created_at < '2022-01-01 00:00:00';
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT REFERENCES test_table(id), value DECIMAL(10, 2));
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, value) VALUES (1, 123.456), (2, NULL), (3, 100.00);
SELECT at.ref_id, initcap(tt.name) AS item_name, at.value FROM another_table at JOIN test_table tt ON at.ref_id = tt.id WHERE NOT (at.value IS NULL) ORDER BY initialized_name DESC;
